acceptanceCriteria: A player can understand the objective from the first screen and complete or fail the loop with real input.
Every high-value visual surface has an asset, authored mesh, or documented fallback with provenance.
Gameplay behavior lives in src/scripts/**/*.ts and every exported system is referenced from structured source.
The scene has authored materials, lighting, camera framing, environment context, and set dressing instead of a placeholder floor and loose primitives.
Proof includes authoring validation, build, playtest motion, screenshot, game score, QA, and release checks.

script.responsibility: throttle/steer profile notes before checkpoint mechanics are added

archetypeDetails.summary: Vehicle games with throttle, steering, and chase-camera framing.

archetypeSuggestions.command: tn actor add vehicle --id plan.vehicle --scene arena --project . --json
archetypeSuggestions.reason: Vehicle goals should start from the bounded arcade vehicle shell instead of raw physics JSON.

archetypeSuggestions.command: tn actor add prop-static --id plan.prop.01 --scene arena --project . --json
archetypeSuggestions.reason: Dominant static set dressing should carry collider/body provenance instead of untracked primitives.

assetPlan.fallback: Author a custom hero mesh with distinct silhouette, material zones, and scale cues.
requiredEvidence: tn asset inspect or model-test result
assetPlan.searchCommand: tn asset source search --game-category racing --format glb --direct-only --json
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Use a direct GLB/glTF returned by the SQLite-backed asset source library before open web research or primitives.

assetPlan.fallback: Author modular hazards or opponents with readable shapes and collision affordances.
assetPlan.searchCommand: tn asset source search --game-category racing --format glb --direct-only --json
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Use the same SQLite catalog kit/style family as the player when possible.

assetPlan.fallback: Create authored collectible/goal meshes with emissive or animated affordance.
assetPlan.searchCommand: tn asset source search --game-category racing --format glb --direct-only --json
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Pick reward/interactable GLB records from the SQLite catalog that read clearly at gameplay camera distance.

assetPlan.fallback: Build a coherent environment from authored meshes, terrain, barriers, landmarks, and sky/background treatment.
assetPlan.searchCommand: tn asset source search --game-category racing --format glb --direct-only --json
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Prefer a consistent SQLite catalog environment pack, not unrelated one-off assets.

assetPlan.fallback: Use retained UI source with authored typography, contrast, spacing, and state-specific labels.
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Use structured UI documents for HUD, pause, fail/retry, win/milestone, loading, settings, and touch controls.

assetPlan.fallback: Use generated or procedural sounds only through local tooling with provenance and fallback notes.
assetPlan.sourcePreference: Plan feedback sounds for input, collect/hit, win/fail, and ambient loop when supported.

controls: touch-control fallback when mobile is in scope
design.failRetry: Define a loss, timeout, hazard, or reset condition and a retained UI retry state.
feedback: camera or VFX emphasis for important actions
design.loop: Spawn, read the objective, act with real input, receive feedback, reach a win/fail state, and retry quickly.
design.objective: Turn 'Reimplement the advanced vehicle course from the inspected prototype baseline with a catalog race car, portable four-wheel drivetrain, mixed asphalt and ice, jump, collision damage, three ordered checkpoints, finish, retry, readable HUD feedback, exact no-throttle causal control, paired web/desktop proof, final polish, clip wiring or explicit no-clip rationale, and relative scale checks.' into one concrete verb, one target, and one measurable success condition.
design.progression: Add at least one escalation such as score, lap, wave, timer, obstacle density, or collectible count.

diagnostics.message: Game plan used fallback camera defaults because the project inventory has no camera entity.
Game plan used fallback player entity defaults because the project inventory has no player-like entity id.

goal: Reimplement the advanced vehicle course from the inspected prototype baseline with a catalog race car, portable four-wheel drivetrain, mixed asphalt and ice, jump, collision damage, three ordered checkpoints, finish, retry, readable HUD feedback, exact no-throttle causal control, paired web/desktop proof, final polish, clip wiring or explicit no-clip rationale, and relative scale checks.

recommendedOperations: Run tn game plan --goal <game idea> --project . --json for the next bounded slice.

acceptanceAssertions.description: Input visibly drives the vehicle through ordered checkpoints.
A timer or checkpoint counter updates during play.
The final checkpoint reaches a finish state.
Retry resets the route or race.

cautions: Use this descriptor as the only source of truth for right/up/forward signs before authoring movement math.
scriptResponsibilities: convert planar input through BasisEx before writing Transform or velocity state

appliesWhen: camera follows player, vehicle, runner, or top-down pawn
cautions: Camera target and offset must be authored source data or pure CameraMath output, not adapter handles.
scriptResponsibilities: keeps target framing and follow offset consistent with authored camera source

cautions: This is kinematic intent, not promoted wheel or drivetrain physics.
Do not read runtime camera or renderer objects; pass input, dt, pose, and velocity as plain data.

appliesWhen: checkpoint, lap, race, delivery-route, and waypoint objectives
cautions: Checkpoint order and lap finish events must come from plain reducer state, not visible mesh order guesses.

acceptanceCriteria: Player reaches checkpoints in order and receives feedback for missed or out-of-order gates.
Scale and camera proof show the vehicle and route are readable.

blocks.summary: Drive or pilot through ordered checkpoints with lap/time progress and missed-checkpoint feedback.

kitCandidates.summary: Checkpoint race loop with ordered goals, readable vehicle scale, camera proof, and HUD progress.

acceptanceCriteria: Player changes lanes through declared input and lane bounds clamp illegal movement.
Forward speed or obstacle density escalates over time.
Obstacle contact or missed timing reaches a fail/retry state.

blocks.summary: Steer between lanes, dodge obstacles, collect bonuses, and track distance/score.

kitCandidates.summary: Lane-based runner loop with hazards, bonuses, escalation, and fail/retry.

acceptanceCriteria: Player moves in a bounded top-down play space using declared input.
Collecting all rewards updates retained UI state and reaches a win state.
Hazards or fail triggers produce a retry path.

blocks.summary: Move a hero, collect rewards, avoid hazards, update score/lives, and win when all rewards are collected.

kitCandidates.summary: Compact top-down collection loop with score, hazards, win, and retry states.

mechanicDecomposition.command: tn recipe apply vehicle-checkpoint --scene arena --vehicle player --camera camera.main --project . --json
mechanicDecomposition.summary: Author continuous input response through portable script state and declared Transform/resource writes.

mechanicDecomposition.command: tn recipe apply vehicle-checkpoint --scene arena --vehicle player --camera camera.main --project . --json
mechanicDecomposition.summary: Track progress, scoring, win/fail state, and retry through source-owned resources and retained UI.

mechanicDecomposition.command: tn flow create plan.match --initial ready --scene arena --project . --json
mechanicDecomposition.summary: Keep menu/play/win/fail/retry state in bounded GameFlow data before adding script-owned state flags.
Keep the player, objective, and feedback moments framed without runtime adapter handles.
Author intro, win, fail, and milestone feedback beats as Sequence tracks before writing cutscene scripts.
Place hazards, rewards, checkpoints, or targets from data so playtests can discover stable IDs.

message: Deterministic game-production plan generated without mutating source.

phases.summary: Prove real input changes game state through a playable loop.
Record local/procedural/generated/blocked asset and audio provenance.
Score art direction, player/world/reward surfaces, materials, lighting, VFX, UI, and performance.
Cover HUD, pause, settings, loading, fail/retry, win/milestone, and touch controls.
Inspect authoring, runtime, browser, and asset diagnostics.
Collect screenshot, mobile, interaction, and performance evidence.
Check build artifacts, bundle budgets, debug helper risks, and residual release blockers.

polishPlan.acceptance: Screenshot shows the player, objective, bounds, and at least one landmark without empty-horizon composition.
polishPlan.treatment: Frame the play space with camera placement, landmarks, readable objective placement, and environment boundaries.

polishPlan.acceptance: Primary surfaces do not read as flat random colors on bare boxes.
polishPlan.treatment: Author material intent with color, roughness/metalness, texture/normal detail where available, and emissive accents only where useful.

polishPlan.acceptance: Gameplay silhouettes remain readable at normal camera distance and motion direction is clear.
polishPlan.treatment: Shape player, hazards, goals, and interactables with distinct proportions, scale, and marker treatment.

polishPlan.acceptance: Scene proof does not show missing shadows, bland floors, or invisible objective cues.
polishPlan.sourceSurface: scene lights, environment, sky/background, and terrain documents
polishPlan.treatment: Use purposeful key/fill/ambient balance, shadows where supported, ground detail, sky/background treatment, and set dressing.

polishPlan.acceptance: Input playtest and recording show smooth response and visible state changes.
polishPlan.treatment: Add eased movement, progress feedback, hit/collect/win/fail cues, and camera/VFX emphasis through portable contracts.

scriptPlan.responsibility: Continue the existing structured-source gameplay system and keep component/resource ownership declared.

supportedShape: Use scene entities, scene-local prefabs, resources, camera, light, MeshRenderer-compatible components, and authored transforms.
Scene-local prefab primitives are limited to box, capsule, cone, cylinder, plane, sphere, torus.
Put gameplay-owned custom component state on entities and reference scripts through content/systems/*.systems.json.
avoid: Unsupported primitive names such as octahedron unless validation proves support.
Raw Three.js scenes, DOM APIs, filesystem access, workers, timers, or runtime handles.
Generated dist/** files as durable source.

supportedShape: Declare actions with string bindings such as keyboard.KeyW, keyboard.ArrowLeft, and keyboard.Space.
Use stable action ids that scripts read through context.input.axis1 or context.input.action/pressed.
avoid: Object-shaped bindings like { device, code }; validation expects non-empty binding strings.
Non-canonical keyboard codes or display labels in place of KeyboardEvent.code names.

supportedShape: Reference src/scripts/**/*.ts module/export pairs from fixedUpdate for input-driven gameplay loops.
Declare every component/resource read and write, including Transform, custom gameplay components, and GameState.
avoid: Leaving reads/writes implicit; effect validation and playtest readiness depend on declared access.
Using update for fixed-time movement unless the game intentionally does not need fixedDelta-driven proof.

supportedShape: Use retained UI nodes with type, text, style, layout, and a bindings array that maps node ids to GameState fields.
Represent gameplay, pause, settings, loading, fail-retry, win-milestone, and touch-controls states.
avoid: roots/children/kind trees when the project uses the current retained UI document shape.
Visible instructional paragraphs; prefer concise HUD state text that fits mobile proof.

supportedShape: Use material rows with id, color, roughness, metalness, emissive, and supported texture-slot fields.
Preserve authored color/material intent in source; fix mapping or runtime setup if screenshots differ.
avoid: baseColor in structured material source; use color for this document family.
Adapter-only color/material tweaks to chase screenshots.

supportedShape: Record asset rows with id, path, and type for source/model/audio/texture/document evidence.
Preserve SQLite catalog ids, source URLs, provenance URLs, license evidence, and fallback notes next to committed assets.
avoid: uri/kind/provenance fields in the asset row shape; put provenance in a source document or notes file.
Web-sourced assets before querying the shipped SQLite asset source catalog.

steps.summary: Create or verify a player verb, objective, input path, camera, and feedback loop.
recipeProofHints: Playtest throttle/steer axes and verify checkpoint trigger progress or lap state.

steps.summary: Add a concrete objective or reward target that changes state.
recipeProofHints: Validate source, build, inspect scene wiring, and run a movement or trigger proof tied to the recipe.

steps.summary: Use when the requested game is a compact top-down collectible loop with score feedback.
recipeProofHints: Playtest MoveX/MoveZ axes and verify HUD score text updates after collection.

steps.summary: Use when the requested game is a lane runner with hazards, jumps, and forward motion.
recipeProofHints: Playtest lateral lane input and jump input; verify fail/retry state changes on hazard trigger.

steps.summary: Use when the requested game centers on a vehicle reaching checkpoint triggers.
recipeProofHints: Playtest throttle/steer axes and verify checkpoint trigger progress or lap state.

steps.summary: Use when the requested game focuses on dodging or timing around clear hazards.
recipeProofHints: Validate source, build, inspect scene wiring, and run a movement or trigger proof tied to the recipe.

steps.summary: Use to add a bounded first pass of ground, landmark, lighting, and material context.
recipeProofHints: Capture screenshot proof with visible ground, landmark, lighting, and scale cues.
recipeScriptResponsibilities: owns declared source mutations and proof evidence for the recipe

steps.summary: Represent gameplay, pause, settings, loading, fail/retry, win/milestone, and touch-control states in retained UI source.
Record local, procedural, generated, hybrid, or blocked sourcing for player/world/reward/UI/audio surfaces.
Collect screenshot, mobile, playtest, performance, and release evidence before claiming done.
